data.title: BMO Capital Initiates Buy on Parker Hannifin Amid Structural Upgrade and Enhanced Earnings Power
data.description:
, an analyst from BMO Capital, has initiated a new Buy rating on Parker Hannifin (PH).

Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

BMO Capital has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors tied to Parker Hannifin’s structural repositioning and earnings power. The firm highlights Parker’s transition into a higher‑quality industrial franchise, with a larger share of its business now tied to secular growth markets and recurring aftermarket revenue that support more durable cash flows and a premium valuation multiple.

BMO Capital’s rating is based on expectations that both cyclical and structural drivers can support upside to estimates and the current share price. Improving indicators in shorter‑cycle end markets, ongoing margin expansion from a disciplined operating culture, and a robust M&A and capital allocation track record underpin their view that the company can continue to outperform despite its strong historical run.

In another report released on March 23, Citi also maintained a Buy rating on the stock with a $1,092.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: iBio (IBIO-610): Validated Activin E Mechanism, Strategic Trial Positioning, and Attractive Risk-Reward Support a Buy Rating
data.description:
Patrick Dolezal, an analyst from LifeSci Capital, maintained the Buy rating on Ibio. The associated price target remains the same with $5.00.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Patrick Dolezal has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors tied to IBIO-610’s differentiated mechanism and favorable positioning. He views the recent WVE-007 results, despite investor disappointment, as validating the broader strategy of targeting activin E, supporting potential applications in combination with incretin therapies, in patients with obesity and type 2 diabetes, and in maintenance settings after initial weight-loss treatment.

He also emphasizes that iBio can leverage extensive emerging clinical data, including Wave’s and Arrowhead’s programs, to refine trial design, patient selection by BMI and visceral fat, and optimal treatment lines. With an IND for IBIO-610 anticipated in Q4 2026, a multi-year cash runway, and a clearly defined development path, he believes the risk-reward profile remains attractive at current valuation levels, supporting a Buy recommendation.

In another report released on March 18, Chardan Capital also maintained a Buy rating on the stock with a $5.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk
